headline: How CEO Transition and Russell Reclassification Will Impact Copart (CPRT) Investors
summary: Copart, Inc. recently announced that CEO Jeff Liaw will step down and former long-time CEO and current Executive Chairman Jay Adair will reassume the chief executive role on July 31, 2026, with Liaw staying on as Special Advisor to support the transition. At the same time, Copart was reclassified across several Russell indices, leaving multiple large-cap growth and value benchmarks and joining the Russell Midcap and Midcap Value benchmarks, highlighting how its evolving profile may alter how...

headline: Copart (CPRT) Stock Looks Cheap On Fair Value While Returns Stay Weak
summary: Copart stock has fallen sharply over the past year, yet the company still screens as undervalued, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate suggesting a material gap to the current share price. That sets up a clear tension for investors between weak recent returns and valuation checks that still lean supportive of Copart. Copart shares are down 39.2% over the past year, which puts the recent valuation debate front and center for existing and potential shareholders. The...
